headline: National Anti-Corruption Plan 2019-2023 met only 41% of targets
context: An independent review of the National Anti-Corruption Plan found that only 41% of its 115 initiatives were fully implemented by the extended deadline of December 2025.

cards.big: National anti-corruption plan achieved just 41% of its 115 targets
cards.sub: Even with a two-year extension, majority of initiatives remain incomplete

cards.big: Plan was launched by Tun Mahathir in January 2019 with 2023 deadline
cards.sub: Three prime ministers later, no single leader has taken ownership of delivery

cards.big: RM420 million allocated to plan implementation over six years
cards.sub: 59% of unimplemented initiatives had fully disbursed budgets despite non-delivery

cards.big: Monitoring committee met only twice in 2025 despite quarterly meeting schedule
cards.sub: High-level oversight collapsed as political attention shifted to other priorities

cards.big: An anti-corruption plan that fails is worse than no plan — it proves the system cannot reform itself
Should the next anti-corruption plan have legally binding milestones with consequences?
